code_issues.issue: No actual Windows Print Agent implementation exists - only server-side handlers are implemented. The user stories require a Windows service that can be installed on client machines.
code_issues.fix: Implement the Windows agent as a Go service that runs on Windows with printer discovery, job polling, and print execution capabilities

code_issues.issue: validateEnrollmentToken only checks if token is non-empty (line 427-428) - no actual token validation against database
code_issues.fix: Implement proper enrollment token validation with database lookup and expiration checks
Use proper configuration management for JWT secrets

code_issues.issue: UpdateStatus function uses SQL string concatenation for timestamp (line 258) - potential SQL injection vulnerability
code_issues.fix: Use proper parameterized query for timestamp field

code_issues.issue: Two different handler files (agent.go and agent_heartbeat.go) with overlapping heartbeat functionality - code duplication
code_issues.fix: Consolidate heartbeat handling into a single handler or clearly separate concerns

code_issues.issue: DocumentChecksum and DocumentSize are hardcoded to empty/0 in AssignedJob response (lines 123-124)
code_issues.fix: Populate these fields from the documents table

code_issues.issue: DatabaseCertificateValidator is in-memory only (line 48-60), not database-backed as name suggests
code_issues.fix: Implement actual database-backed certificate validation or rename to InMemoryCertificateValidator

code_issues.issue: ExtractIDFromPath functions use custom string parsing (line 622-667) instead of using standard routing libraries like chi or gorilla/mux
code_issues.fix: Consider using a proper routing library for cleaner path extraction

missing_items: Windows service implementation (agent.exe) that runs as a Windows service
Windows installer (MSI) with silent installation support
Printer discovery implementation using Windows API (Get-Printer, WMI, etc.)
Print execution using Windows print API/commands
Agent configuration via registry or config file
Windows Event Log integration for installation logs
Auto-start on Windows boot as Windows service
Network change detection for triggering printer rediscovery
Unit tests for agent handlers (agent_discovery, agent_heartbeat, agent_poll)
Integration tests for complete agent registration to print execution flow
Documentation for agent deployment and configuration
blocking_issues: No Windows agent executable exists - the entire client-side component is missing
MSI installer for silent installation does not exist
Printer discovery implementation that actually discovers Windows printers does not exist
Print execution capability does not exist
fix_instructions:
1. Create a new Go project for the Windows agent under cmd/agent or services/agent
2. Implement Windows service wrapper using golang.org/x/sys/windows/svc package
3. Implement printer discovery using Windows API or PowerShell Get-Printer command
4. Implement print execution using Windows print commands or API
5. Create MSI installer using WiX or similar tooling
6. Add unit tests for all agent handlers
7. Fix SQL injection vulnerability in UpdateStatus function
8. Implement proper enrollment token validation
9. Move hardcoded secrets to configuration
10. Add integration tests for complete flow
